title: Amsterdam, Paris, Lucerne & Italy Grand Tour.

trains.name: Trains & Taxi Transfers
trains.blurb: High-speed trains and private transfers link four countries, from Amsterdam's canals to Rome's ruins.
trains.route: Amsterdam (2N) → Paris (2N) → Lucerne (3N) → Mestre Venice (2N) → Florence (1N) → Rome (2N)

transport.1: Amsterdam airport to Amsterdam hotel private transfer.
transport.2: Full-day coach tour to the windmills of Zaanse Schans and the Volendam cheese factory, 5.5 hours.
transport.3: Amsterdam to Paris train ticket, II Class.
transport.4: Paris city tour by coach with a Seine river cruise and a visit to the Eiffel Tower's 2nd floor, 4 hours.
transport.5: Paris to Lucerne train ticket, II Class, with a 4-day consecutive Swiss Travel Pass beginning today.
transport.6: Full-day excursion to Mount Titlis on the Swiss Travel Pass, including a complimentary Lake Lucerne cruise.

inclusions: Amsterdam airport to hotel private transfer
Windmills, Zaanse Schans and Volendam cheese factory coach tour, 5.5 hrs
Amsterdam to Paris train ticket, II Class
Paris city tour, Seine cruise and Eiffel Tower 2nd floor visit, 4 hrs coach tour
Paris to Lucerne train ticket, II Class
4-day consecutive Swiss Travel Pass, II Class
Mount Titlis excursion with pass, Lake Lucerne cruise included

private.name: Private Exclusive Coach
private.blurb: A private coach and driver stay with you from Amsterdam to Rome, with guided stops along the way.
private.route: Amsterdam (2N) → Paris (2N) → Lucerne (3N) → Mestre Venice (2N) → Florence (1N) → Rome (2N)

transport.1: Amsterdam airport to Amsterdam hotel private transfer; private coach at your disposal from Day 1 through Day 13.
transport.2: Private canal cruise combined with a Volendam and Marken excursion.
transport.4: Paris panoramic tour with a private English-speaking guide, 3 hours, plus a 1-hour Seine river cruise.
transport.6: Excursion to Titlis and Engelberg by private vehicle.
transport.9: Venice walking tour, 2 hours, with a private English-speaking guide.
transport.12: Rome city tour with a private English-speaking guide, 3 hours.
transport.13: Private coach transfer from Rome hotel to Rome airport, concluding the private vehicle service (Day 1-13).

inclusions: Private coach on disposal from Day 1 till Day 13
Amsterdam airport to hotel private transfer
Canal cruise combined with Volendam and Marken excursion
Paris panoramic tour with English-speaking guide, 3 hrs
Titlis and Engelberg excursion by private vehicle
Venice walking tour, 2 hrs with English-speaking guide
Rome city tour with English-speaking guide, 3 hrs

days.title: Arrive Amsterdam
days.desc: Arrive into the Dutch capital, a city defined by its concentric 17th-century canal ring and Golden Age merchant houses. After the transfer to your hotel, the rest of the day is free to explore the nearby streets on foot or by bike.
days.taste: A glass of jenever and a plate of bitterballen at In 't Aepjen, one of Amsterdam's oldest bars dating to 1519, is the quintessential first-evening ritual.

days.title: Windmills & Volendam
days.desc: A full day outside the city: the working windmills and green-painted warehouses of Zaanse Schans, followed by the traditional fishing village of Volendam with its wooden-clog workshops and harbourside houses, and a stop at a working cheese farm.
days.taste: Fresh Edam and Gouda cheese tasting at the Volendam cheese factory.

days.title: Amsterdam to Paris
days.desc: Travel by high-speed train from Amsterdam Centraal to Paris, crossing the Dutch and Belgian countryside before arriving in the French capital by afternoon. Settle into your hotel with the evening free to enjoy the city's illuminated boulevards.

days.title: Paris
days.desc: A full day discovering Paris: the grand avenues around the Arc de Triomphe and Champs-Élysées, a cruise along the Seine past Notre-Dame and the Louvre, and a visit to the Eiffel Tower's second floor for sweeping views over the city.
days.experience: An evening Seine river cruise past the illuminated monuments is one of Paris's classic experiences.
days.taste: Steak-frites and a glass of Bordeaux at a Left Bank bistro, or macarons from Ladurée on the Champs-Élysées, are the classic Parisian way to end the day.
days.shopping: The Champs-Élysées and the Galeries Lafayette department store, with its stained-glass cupola on Boulevard Haussmann, are famed Paris shopping destinations.

days.title: Paris to Lucerne
days.desc: Travel by train from Paris into Switzerland, arriving in Lucerne on the shores of Lake Lucerne beneath the snow-capped peaks of Mount Pilatus and Rigi. Explore the medieval Old Town and the covered Chapel Bridge in the evening light.

days.title: Mount Titlis excursion
days.desc: A full-day excursion to Mount Titlis, reached by revolving cable car to 3,020 metres, where a glacier, the Cliff Walk suspension bridge and an ice grotto await above the clouds. Return via a scenic cruise on Lake Lucerne.
days.experience: The rotating Titlis cable car and the glacier ice grotto are among Switzerland's most memorable high-alpine experiences.

days.title: Lucerne at leisure
days.desc: A free day in Lucerne to explore at your own pace: the wooden Chapel Bridge and its octagonal water tower, the carved Lion Monument mourning the Swiss Guard, and the lakeside promenade lined with Belle Époque hotels.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — optional lake cruise or mountain excursion available.
days.taste: Luzerner Chügelipastete, the city's signature veal-and-mushroom vol-au-vent, and Swiss chocolate from Confiserie Bachmann, Lucerne's own chocolatier since 1852, are worth seeking out.

days.title: Lucerne to Venice (Mestre)
days.desc: Travel south across the Alps via the Gotthard route, one of Europe's most scenic rail and road corridors, descending into the Italian plain and on to Mestre, the gateway town for Venice on the mainland.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — self-arranged travel to Venice today.

days.title: Venice
days.desc: A guided walking tour through Venice's historic centre: St Mark's Square and Basilica, the Doge's Palace, and the winding canals and bridges of the sestieri, crossing the Rialto Bridge over the Grand Canal.
days.experience: A gondola ride along the quieter canals is the definitive Venetian experience.
days.taste: Cicchetti and a Select spritz at the bacari around the Rialto Market, or a coffee at Caffè Florian in St Mark's Square, the oldest coffee house in the world, dating to 1720.

days.title: Venice to Florence
days.desc: Travel through the Veneto and into Tuscany, arriving in Florence, cradle of the Renaissance. Spend the evening strolling past the Duomo's marble facade and across the Ponte Vecchio over the Arno.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — self-arranged travel to Florence today.

days.title: Florence to Rome
days.desc: A morning free in Florence near the Uffizi Gallery and the Piazza della Signoria before travelling south through the Tuscan and Umbrian hills to Rome, the Eternal City, in time for an evening stroll near the Trevi Fountain.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — self-arranged travel to Rome today.
days.taste: A scoop of gelato at Vivoli, Florence's oldest gelateria since 1930, or bistecca alla fiorentina at a Mercato Centrale stall, are classic Florentine tastes before heading south.

days.title: Rome
days.desc: A guided city tour of Rome taking in the Colosseum and the Roman Forum, the Pantheon's ancient dome, and the baroque Trevi Fountain and Spanish Steps, tracing more than two thousand years of history.
days.taste: Classic Roman dishes such as carbonara and cacio e pepe, followed by gelato at Giolitti, Rome's historic gelateria dating to 1900 near the Pantheon, are not to be missed.

days.title: Depart Rome
days.desc: Free time until your transfer to Rome airport, depending on flight schedule, bringing the journey from Amsterdam to Rome to a close.

about.title: Best season
about.body: April to June and September to October offer mild weather across all four countries and thinner crowds than midsummer.

about.title: Weather
about.body: Expect cooler, changeable conditions in Amsterdam, Paris and Lucerne (10-22°C) and warmer Mediterranean conditions in Florence and Rome (15-30°C in summer).

about.title: Currency & docs
about.body: The Netherlands, France, Italy and Vatican-adjacent Rome use the Euro (€), but Switzerland uses the Swiss Franc (CHF) despite being within the Schengen border-free area, so a small amount of local currency for Lucerne is worth carrying.

goodToKnow.title: Tickets & timing
goodToKnow.body: Book the Eiffel Tower, Uffizi Gallery and Colosseum slots ahead in summer — all three sell out days in advance. Mount Titlis is quietest on the first cable car up.

goodToKnow.title: Rail travel
goodToKnow.body: Seat reservations are included on the Amsterdam-Paris and Paris-Lucerne trains. The Swiss Travel Pass also covers city transport and museum discounts in Lucerne.

goodToKnow.title: Your vehicle
goodToKnow.body: Driver working hours are limited to 10 hrs per day by law, so long transfer days such as Lucerne to Venice are paced with rest stops. Luggage space fits one large case per guest.

goodToKnow.title: Border crossings
goodToKnow.body: Switzerland is in the Schengen area but not the EU, so a currency change is the main practical difference — no passport control is expected at the borders.

goodToKnow.title: What to pack
goodToKnow.body: Layers for alpine evenings in Lucerne, comfortable shoes for cobblestones in Florence and Rome, and a light rain layer for Amsterdam and Paris.

goodToKnow.title: Money
goodToKnow.body: Cards are accepted almost everywhere; keep small Euro and Swiss Franc cash amounts for the Volendam clog and cheese stalls, Lucerne's lakeside kiosks, and Venice's bacari, which often prefer cash for a quick round of cicchetti.

terms: All rates are net and per person for the package.
Child rates apply for children aged 2 to 11 years old. One child sharing a room with 2 adults, subject to hotel policy.
All rates are subject to availability at the time of booking. Europe Incoming will endeavour to match the rates quoted.
Rates are not applicable during trade fair periods, major European public holidays, and other major events.
City taxes are not included in the price.
If certain attractions are closed during specific periods, alternative options will be arranged.
All bookings must be confirmed at least 60 working days prior to arrival.
100% pre-payment required by bank transfer or credit card (Visa or Mastercard). All bank transfer charges covered by the Agent.
Vouchers will be issued after receipt of full payment.
